CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder RESULT result.

    METHODS rba_Items FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys_rba FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder\_Items FULL result_requested RESULT result LINK association_links.

    METHODS cba_Items FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities_cba FOR CREATE ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder\_Items.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchaseOrder IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD create.

    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_header_create>).

      lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_header = VALUE #( doc_type     = 'ZPO'
                                               purch_org    = <ls_header_create>-PurchasingOrganization
                                               pur_group    = <ls_header_create>-PurchasingGroup
                                               vendor       = <ls_header_create>-Supplier
                                               comp_code    = <ls_header_create>-CompanyCode
                                               currency     = <ls_header_create>-DocumentCurrency
                                               pmnttrms     = <ls_header_create>-PaymentTerms ).

      lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_headerx = VALUE #( doc_type    = abap_true
                                                purch_org   = abap_true
                                                pur_group   = abap_true
                                                vendor      = abap_true
                                                comp_code   = abap_true
                                                currency    = abap_true
                                                pmnttrms    = abap_true ).

      EXIT. "we expect always 1 header to come
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD read.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD rba_Items.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cba_Items.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS read FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem RESULT result.

    METHODS rba_Header FOR READ
      IMPORTING keys_rba FOR READ ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem\_Header FULL result_requested RESULT result LINK association_links.

    METHODS create FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING entities FOR CREATE ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZC_LeanRAP_PurchOrdItem IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD read.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD rba_Header.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create.
    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_item_create>).

      APPEND VALUE bapimepoitem( po_item    = sy-tabix
                                item_cat    =  '0'
                                acctasscat  = 'C'
                                plant       = <ls_item_create>-Plant
                                short_text  = <ls_item_create>-PurchaseOrderItemText
                                quantity    = <ls_item_create>-OrderQuantity
                                po_unit     = <ls_item_create>-PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit
                                net_price   = <ls_item_create>-NetPriceAmount
                                matl_group  = 'TEST' )
                   TO lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_item.

      APPEND VALUE bapimepoitem( po_item    = sy-tabix
                               item_cat     =  abap_true
                               acctasscat   = abap_true
                               plant        = abap_true
                               short_text   = abap_true
                               quantity     = abap_true
                               po_unit      = abap_true
                               net_price    = abap_true
                               matl_group   = 'TEST' )
                  TO lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_itemx.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_ZC_LEANRAP_PURCHASEORDER DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_saver.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

    METHODS finalize REDEFINITION.

    METHODS check_before_save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS save REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup REDEFINITION.

    METHODS cleanup_finalize REDEFINITION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lsc_ZC_LEANRAP_PURCHASEORDER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD check_before_save.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save.

    DATA ls_new_po TYPE bapimepoheader.
    DATA lt_return TYPE bapiret2_t.

    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CREATE1'
      EXPORTING
        poheader  = lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_header
        poheaderx = lhc_buffer=>ss_bapi_po_headerx
        testrun   = abap_false
      IMPORTING
        expheader = ls_new_po
      TABLES
        return    = lt_return
        poitem    = lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_item
        poitemx   = lhc_buffer=>st_bapi_po_itemx.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD cleanup_finalize.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

 

In the next part(s) of this blog series we will take a look at adding more features while sticking to "lean RAP" recommendations:

- implementing error handling and late numbering

- updating both header and item fields for existing Purchase Orders (changesets)

- adding new items to existing Purchase Order with "create by association" operation

- implementing concurrency handling with etags

Hope this blog series will help a few more fellow developers to transition from SEGW and DPC_EXT classes to RAP ;-).

entry.title: My Journey Through Devtoberfest 2024: Learning, Connecting, and Growing with SAP
entry.content:
English Version.

After returning from my September vacation, I started seeing LinkedIn posts about Devtoberfest 2024. Although I’d heard about it in previous years, I had never taken the time to look into whether it could actually help me in the future.

After reading one of those posts, I decided to register and experience it firsthand to see what it was all about, what activities it offered, and how it could benefit my future in SAP.

So, I signed up and prepared myself for the four weeks of Devtoberfest, with the goal of exploring how far I could go. During the event, there was a break for the Teched online, another key week for learning about SAP's future.

Each day of the week was dedicated to a specific topic:

  • Monday: ABAP & CAP
  • Tuesday: Tooling
  • Wednesday: Integration
  • Thursday: MAD (Machine Learning, AI, and Data)
  • Friday: Frontend

On September 23, the activities for the first week began. Although I wasn’t entirely sure what the event or the activity dynamics would be like, I was determined to seize the opportunity and learn about the latest SAP innovations.

Before each week ended, the activities for the following week would be unlocked, with links provided for easy access to each one. There were also video sessions featuring SAP experts sharing their knowledge, followed by questions to reinforce learning. However, what I enjoyed most were the Friday activities, which were designed to foster community interaction: sharing photos of our pets, our workspaces, or finding bugs in different languages, and even uploading Halloween-themed photos.

In conclusion, I think anyone in the field of SAP development should participate in Devtoberfest at least once. It’s a very well-organized event with a lot to learn. Personally, I discovered tools like SAP BUILD, which allows the creation of applications, workflows, and forms without needing coding skills — the trendy Low-code No-code approach. I also learned about Clean Core, ABAP for Cloud, Integration Suite, and Fiori for the frontend. These four weeks not only helped me improve my skills but also allowed me to connect with the community and meet SAP specialists.

A big thank you to @thomas_jung  for organizing the event, to @ajmaradiaga for his posts that motivated me to participate in Devtoberfest 2024, and to all the experts who shared their knowledge.

I finished the event with a score of 45,710 points, and now I eagerly await to see if I'll be one of the lucky ones to receive a special prize!

Spanish Version.

Después de las vacaciones de septiembre, empecé a ver publicaciones en LinkedIn sobre el Devtoberfest 2024. Aunque había oído hablar de él en años anteriores, nunca me había detenido a investigar si realmente podría ayudarme en el futuro.

Al leer una de esas publicaciones, decidí inscribirme y experimentarlo desde dentro para ver en qué consistía, qué actividades ofrecía y cómo podría beneficiarme en mi futuro en SAP.

Así que me apunté y me preparé para las cuatro semanas del Devtoberfest, con la intención de explorar hasta dónde podría llegar. Durante el evento, hubo una pausa para el Teched online, otra semana clave para conocer el futuro de SAP.

Cada día de la semana estaba dedicado a un tema específico:

  • Lunes: ABAP & CAP
  • Martes: Tooling
  • Miércoles: Integration
  • Jueves: MAD (Machine Learning, AI and Data)
  • Viernes: Frontend

El 23 de septiembre comenzaron las actividades de la primera semana. Aunque no tenía claro cómo sería el evento ni las dinámicas de las actividades, estaba decidido a aprovechar la oportunidad y aprender las novedades que SAP ofrece.

Antes de finalizar cada semana, se activaban las actividades de la siguiente, proporcionando enlaces para acceder a cada una sin problemas. También había sesiones en video con expertos de SAP que compartían su conocimiento, seguidas de preguntas para reforzar el aprendizaje. Sin embargo, lo que más disfruté fueron las actividades de los viernes, diseñadas para fomentar la interacción en la comunidad: compartir fotos con nuestras mascotas, de nuestros espacios de trabajo o buscar errores en diferentes lenguajes, y hasta subir fotos temáticas de Halloween.

En conclusión, creo que cualquier persona en el ámbito de desarrollo en SAP debería participar al menos una vez en el Devtoberfest. Es un evento muy bien organizado donde se aprende muchísimo. Yo, personalmente, descubrí herramientas como SAP BUILD, que permite crear aplicaciones, flujos de trabajo y formularios sin necesidad de programación, el Low-code No code de moda. Además, aprendí sobre el Clean Core, ABAP para Cloud, Integration Suite, y Fiori para el frontend. Estas cuatro semanas me permitieron no solo mejorar mis habilidades, sino también conectar con la comunidad y conocer a especialistas de SAP.

Gracias a @thomas_jung  por la organización del evento, a @ajmaradiaga  por sus publicaciones que me motivaron a participar en Devtoberfest 2024, y a todos los expertos que compartieron su conocimiento.

Finalicé el evento con una puntuación de 45,710 puntos, ¡y ahora espero con emoción si seré uno de los afortunados en recibir un premio especial!

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

As follow-up to our previous release 2408, this blog post provides an update concerning our most recent release 2411, which is available for customers and partners since November 16th. This particular release features highlights such as ABAP Cloud in SAP Build, a new approach to build Application Jobs, and the region-less subscription of the Landscape Portal.

The complete list of all new features can be found on the What´s New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment page.

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Creating an ABAP Cloud Project in the Context of the SAP BTP ABAP environment, a service key is no longer needed when creating an ABAP Cloud Project. You now enter the ABAP Service instance URL (details)
  • Triggering Where-Used List for Usages of CDS Annotation to enable the investigation in which ABAP CDS an annotation is used
  • Displaying Field Characteristics for RAP Types in the Element Information Popup, The Element Information popup (F2) for RAP types shows additional field characteristics information such as mandatory:create, mandatory:execute or readonly (details)
  • Multi-Dimensional Analysis App Generator, you can now use the multi-dimensional analysis app generator wizard to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a multi-dimensional analysis Fiori app (details)
  • Support of SAP Fiori launchpad app descriptor items in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these items describe how an application is started and consist of a target mapping and one or more tiles, for more information see SAP Fiori Applications in the ABAP Environment and Working with Launchpad App Descriptor Items
  • Rerunning Unit Tests with Coverage in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • Clearing Coverage results, you can now clear your coverage results in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • ABAP Test Cockpit: ATC Check "Usage of Released APIs" can be run on Key User and ABAP Cloud objects

Reuse Services

  • New Class Interface for Application Job Catalog Entries and Templates, with the new attribute-based class interface it is now possible to change the parameters, sections, and groups of a catalog entry (details)
  • Business Rule Framework (BRF plus) is enhanced with a code generation service and an API introduced to facilitate the consumption of business rules within ABAP Cloud (details)
  • XCO XSLX: You can now style the cells of an Excel worksheet. You can also modify existing documents (details)
  • XCO CDS: You can now generate CDS external entities and logical external schemas, and you can now add comments and arithmetic expressions to CDS views (details)

UI Services and SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Creating Launchpad App Descriptor Items (details)

Integration

  • With Business Event Logging, you can capture and log business events locally within the ABAP environment
  • App Display Business Event Logs. It provides an overview of logged business events for different objects and event types. You can explore the details of the event with this app
  • App Display Business Events by Objects: It groups business events by instances of objects and object components
  • App Display Changes to Objects: You can see the changes for your objects on a field level as recorded in business event logging

Security

  • ABAP environment tenants are now equipped with a signed default client certificate, which is automatically rotated and can be used for customer managed integration scenarios
  • The communication scenario SAP_COM_0915 provides access to APIs to read the Read Access Logs from the system, the API can for instance be leveraged by a Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) system
  • TLS 1.3, we now support Transport Layer Security 1.3 for outbound HTTP, WebSocket RFC, and SMTP protocols
  • The system exposes APIs to retrieve customer-managed security settings in a read-only manner

Custom Code Migration App

  • Include ADT links in SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check Export, the export of ATC results for the SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check now includes HTTP(S) links, which enables you to navigate to the source code of example code in ADT for Eclipse. This new functionality is available in the Custom Code Migration app only (details)
  • New option to start analysis after project creation, you can now choose whether you would like to start the custom code analysis run automatically at project creation or whether you would like to schedule it manually after project creation (details)
  • Changes in Scope Calculation Automation, you now need to initiate scope calculation manually after project creation, the scope calculation no longer starts automatically at project creation (details)

Cloud ATC

  • Change Validity Date for ATC Exemptions in the Approve ATC Exemptions App (details)
  • Replication of ATC Exemptions from on-premise towards SAP BTP, Customers need to use transaction SATC_EXPORT_XMPT to download the exemptions from an on-premise ATC system (details)

Technical Monitoring

  • Enhanced app HANA Table Analysis: With the new Column Loads and Column Unloads screens in the HANA Table Analysis app, you can check how many columns of the tables in your HANA database have been loaded to or unloaded from the main storage (details)
  • New app HANA Memory Analysis: With the new app, you can get an overview of the memory consumption of the HANA memory components over time. This helps you to identify bottlenecks in your HANA memory (details)

Lifecycle Management

  • When cloning a software component, you can now optionally use the newly added parameters to request cloning a Bring Your Own Git (BYOG) repository (details)

abapGit

  • Support of object deletions, it can now be exported from and imported to the ABAP environment with abapGit (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Extended Access, you can now access and subscribe to the Landscape Portal for SAP BTP ABAP Environment from every region that is available for the ABAP environment. In regions without SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, some applications in the Landscape Portal are limited or not available (details)
  • Deinstalling Products, the new Undeploy Product app in the Landscape Portal enables you to permanently deinstall existing products (details)

entry.title: ABAP News for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2411
entry.content:
In this blog, you'll learn about the new features for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2411.

News for ABAP Release 9.13

ABAP CDS

Client Safety of CDS Table Functions

CDS table functions can be declared explicitly client-safe using the annotation ClientHandling.clientSafe.

  • More about client safety of CDS table functions
  • Blog about client safety of CDS table functions

Internal Tables

READ TABLE ... WHERE ...

The READ TABLE statement now supports the WHERE addition. If the WHERE condition is fulfilled, the first line of the internal table is read. You can use the following syntax:

LOOP AT itab … WHERE log_exp|(cond_syntax).

More about the WHERE addition in READ TABLE

IS [NOT] INITIAL in WHERE Condition of FILTER

You can now use IS INITIAL or IS NOT INITIAL in the WHERE condition of FILTER.

More about IS [NOT] INITIAL in WHERE condition of FILTER

ABAP SQL

Binary Floating Point Types in Arithmetic Expressions

In ABAP SQL arithmetic expressions, you can now use operands of type FLTP or f in decimal floating point expressions. Integer types can be used in binary floating point expressions.

More about binary floating point types in arithmetic expressions

Transformations

Regular Expressions for XSLT

XSL Transformations now support the regular expression functions matches(), replace(), and tokenize().

  • More about regular expressions for XSLT
  • Blog about regular expressions for XSLT

ABAP Keyword Documentation

The first step has been taken, and starting with this release, we will publish the ABAP Keyword Documentation content with the ADT design. With the ADT design, the documentation looks better, but the feeling remains the same. Sections in the documentation are now even more visible with different recurring design patterns. In addition, the new version improves the search with a type-ahead functionality and a better way to send links (you can copy the URL). In future releases, the ADT design will be replaced by the UI5 design.

  • More about the ADT design

If you have any questions, feel free to comment below. We hope you enjoy the new features.

entry.title: Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance

We are excited to announce the release of the new version of the Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance (MDG), which has recently been published on Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

This updated version includes new solution variants for SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 in classic mode and in cloud-ready mode. One of the key highlights of this release is the inclusion of detailed diagrams for business partner, product, and financial domain for master data governance in classic mode. These diagrams provide a comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, making it easier for users to understand how MDG fits into their existing IT landscape and how it can be integrated with other systems and applications.  

Updates in Detail

1. Solution Variant “Federated Master Data Governance with Core Data Owner on SAP S/4HANA”

We added the information that Federated Master Data Governance can also be used with MDG on lower releases/classic-mode. More information can be found in the how-to guide and in the blog "Consumption of Federated Governance Made Easy" from @Mark63.

In the next two solution variants, we have replaced the existing one for “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA” with two new solution variants to illustrate the supported processes: 

2. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Classic Mode”

In the Solution Value Flow Diagram, you can see that MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode supports multiple domains, including business partner, product, and financials. This diagram provides a clear representation of how a business process variant is implemented and supported by a specific solution. 

As example you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners. In the case of Decentral Creation, a consolidation process is used, whereas for Decentral Change, the MDG, Central Governance Change Request is used. 

The descriptive text next to the diagram contains more information: It provides details about the standard delivered process template and change request types that are suitable for decentralized creation and change processes.  

3. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Cloud-Ready Mode”

In comparison to the Solution Variant for MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode you can see the Solution Value Flow Diagram for cloud-ready mode currently only contains the Business Partner Domain. 

The Solution Process Flow Diagrams were initially published last year. We have now included three new diagrams, which demonstrate the support for Mass Load, Mass Change, and Mass Deletion of Business Partner Relationships. 

Furthermore, to draw a comparison with classic mode diagrams, here you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners in cloud-ready mode. In both cases, consolidation processes are used. 

We believe that this latest release will be a valuable resource for organizations looking to implement or expand their MDM capabilities. By providing a clear and comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, we aim to empower users to make the most of their MDM investment and to achieve their data management goals more efficiently.  

We invite you to explore the new Reference Solution Architecture for MDM by yourself on the Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

Further information

Curious about the initially released content and about the different diagram types such as Solution Value Flow diagram, Solution Component diagram, Solution Data Flow diagram, Solution Process Flow diagram? 

  • Check the blog post “Explore Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance” from my colleague @KarinBlank. 
  • Watch our Video: How to use Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance - YouTube 

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, join the Questions & Answers forum and Blog space, and check the MDG roadmap for all MDG versions. 

Best, Christina Lindner

entry.title: End-To-End Creation of View Cluster
entry.content:
View Cluster: 

View Cluster is a group of maintenance dialogs which are collected in maintenance unit for business or technical reasons. 

The main purpose is that view cluster allows end users to maintain the complex cross-table data and ensure consistency of the data. 

SE54 is the transaction code for creating the View Cluster. 

SM34 is the transaction code to maintain entries in View Cluster. 

Let's Create View cluster using tables SCARS, SPFLI and SFLIGHT. 

First, we need to create maintenance views for tables SCARS, SPFLI and SFLIGHT. 

Create maintainace view for SCARR. 

Step 1: Go to SE11 and create a maintainace view for SCARR. 

Step 2 : Give description and table with which you want to create maintenance view. 

Step 3: Click on view table. 

Step 4 : Click on table fields and select the fields you want. 

Step 5: Click on Maintenance status and select the option you want for access and save and activate. 

Step 6: Go to Utilities and click on table maintenance generator and create a TMG for it. 

Step 7: Provide the function group, Authorization group and maintenance screens and click on create. 

So, maintenance view is created for Scarr (Airline) Table, similarly, need to create maintenance view for SPFLI and Flight table also. 

 

Note: 1. For SPFLI while creating the Maintenance view, once you select the fields from the table you will get the screen like below. 

In the tab view Fields Give ‘S’ in the column ‘P’ for the field Carrid. 

Note: 2. While creating Maintenance view for SFLIGHT 

Follow the same procedure as before for creating maintenance view for SFLIGHT. 

In view fields in the column ‘P’ provide ‘S’ for the fields CARRID AND CONNID as below. 

Once Maintenance view for all the above tables is created Goto SE54 Tcode and create the view cluster as below. 

Steps Create View cluster in SE54. 

Step 1: Goto SE54 and click on Edit View Cluster. 

Step 2: Click on view cluster name and click on Create/Change button. 

Step 3: Provide a short description and save. 

Step 4: Double Click on Object Structure in the left side. 

Step 5: Click on the New Entries button in the toolbar menu. 

Step 6: Enter View/Table, Short text and precedes, Dep,pos,Start. 

The Dep column of the view cluster explains the details of the entry. 

R = Header Entry. 

S = Dependent entry. 

The POS column explains the sequence of the views. 

Start column explains the first view in hierarchy, which is in ZVSCARR in this case. 

Enter the data shown below and click on the Enter Key. 

Step 7: Click on enter. 

Then provide the next view name ZVSPFLI and in the Dep column of it the value of ZSPFLI is ‘S’ because it is dependent on the predecessor view ZVSCARR, and value of predecessor is ZVSCARR. 

Then again do the same procedure for ZVSFLIGHT also in Dep column of it the value of ZVSFLIGHT is ‘S’ because it is dependent on the predecessor view ZVSPFLI, and value of predecessor is ZVSPFLI. 

Step 8: Now Select each line and click on the field dependent button, a popup appears saying ‘Field Dependencies successfully created’ Confirm information popup with enter key or click green tick. 

Similarly do the same things to remaining View/table also. 

Step 9: Once it is done for all the views, click on Header entry/goback to the main screen and click activate button. 

 

 Till now we created the View Cluster. 

Maintaining View Cluster in SM34 

Step 1: Now Go to SM34 and Enter view cluster name and click on Display/Maintenance button. 

In the first screen all the existing entries of the table SCARR would appear. 

First Screen. 

Select the record in the table scarr and click on table SPFLI. 

You will get all the records related to carrid AA. 

Select particular Connid from SPFLI View and click on table Flight you will get all the entries related to selected connid as below.

entry.title: Migrating SAP Smart forms to Adobe Forms
entry.content:
SAP Smart Forms and Adobe Forms are two popular technologies for designing and generating business documents like invoices, purchase orders, and delivery notes. As organizations modernize their SAP landscapes, there is a growing need to migrate from Smart Forms to Adobe Forms to leverage advanced features, better performance, and an enhanced user experience. This document gives an idea about migration of a smart form to an interactive form. 

Why Migrate to Adobe Forms?

  1. Enhanced User Experience:
    • Adobe Forms offer dynamic layouts and interactivity (e.g., input fields, drop-downs).
  2. Modern Integration:
    • Seamless integration with Fiori apps and SAP S/4HANA.
  3. Rich Features:
    • Advanced graphics, barcodes, and accessibility features.
  4. Global Standards:
    • Support for PDF/A compliance for archiving and digital signatures.
  5. Future-Proof:
    • SAP is focusing more on Adobe Forms as a long-term solution in its product roadmap.

Migration Challenges

Migrating from Smart Forms to Adobe Forms involves technical and functional considerations:

  • Layout Redesign: Smart Forms and Adobe Forms have different layout tools and paradigms.
  • Data Binding: Adobe Forms use context nodes and data connections, requiring re-mapping of data.
  • Functional Enhancements: Migrating is an opportunity to enhance existing forms with interactive features.
  • Learning Curve: Development in Adobe Forms requires knowledge of Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

Steps to Migrate

Step1: Go to transaction code SMARTFORMS and select the smart form to be converted into print adobe form. 

 

Here the form name is ZSRS_SF_TEST. 

Step2: Go to the next screen by clicking display button. 

A text element and table are defined in this existing smart form. 

Step3: Output of the smart form ZSRS_SF_TEST is:

Step4: Now go to Utilities-àMigration--àInteractive Form-àExport in the initial screen of the smart form as shown below. 

You will get popup.

Step10:  Click on change button and following screen appears. 

Delete the unnecessary empty folder from the context menu.

Now,
